justification: All pass criteria met: 12-month growth projection calculated (~8.9x to 445,800 orders/day); database identified as primary bottleneck; 85% disk utilization flagged as critical with ~8-week timeline; index fix identified as highest ROI first step; phased plan (indexes → read replicas → partitioning/archival); sharding explicitly deferred ('Do NOT attempt sharding in the next 12 months'); trade-offs include replication lag and cache invalidation complexity.

observations.text: Excellent month-by-month projection table showing orders/day, DB size, CPU estimates with clear inflection points marked
Disk emergency correctly identified as critical risk with actionable mitigation (expand to 1TB immediately)
Full table scans correctly identified as root cause; concrete SQL provided for index creation using CONCURRENTLY
Phased plan is well-structured with clear timelines, engineer allocation, and expected impact metrics
Explicitly warns against sharding, Elasticsearch, and other over-engineering for a 4-engineer team
Trade-offs table covers replication lag, cache invalidation, and single-writer ceiling with alternatives considered
Practical pitfalls section (no DELETE for archival, no sync materialized view refresh) shows operational depth
DB size projection at month 12 is 2.7TB rather than ~3.6TB (400GB × 9); the estimate likely accounts for archival but doesn't explain the discrepancy
Concerns section appropriately emphasizes urgency and team capacity constraints
